prompt:
The nightly inventory sheet is coming out mangled and warehouse ops keeps printing it anyway, which is how I ended up with a laminated piece of garbage on my desk this morning. The header and the dashed rule line print fine, but every DATA row is a mess: the part number column runs straight into the description, the description runs into the bin column, and the quantity ends up welded onto the end of other text — the columns basically don't exist anymore and the lines are way longer than the 57 characters they're supposed to be. It's every row, every run, both on my machine and on the box that generates the sheet. Descriptions that get cut to fit their column also look subtly off to me, but honestly it's hard to tell with everything smashed together.

The layout is supposed to be dead simple and hasn't changed in a year: SKU 10 wide, DESCRIPTION 24, BIN 8, QTY 6 right-aligned, columns joined with " | ", long text just gets cut at the column edge, and a count too big for six digits shows ###### like the spreadsheets do.

Please find whatever is wrong and fix it properly — I don't want the sheet hand-patched into shape, I want the formatter actually correct. `make test` has the layout pinned line-by-line and must pass when you're done. Makefile, mintest.h, test_report.c are off-limits.
